description: A city of contradictions — gleaming malls adjacent to historic Spanish colonial districts, extraordinary Filipino hospitality, a food scene that blends Malay, Chinese, Spanish, and American influences, and a gateway to 7,000+ islands that will blow your mind.

neighborhoodRecs: BGC (Bonifacio Global City) — Safest, most walkable, international restaurants, nightlife in High Street, clean and modern.
Makati CBD — Business district, excellent restaurants, Greenbelt mall, safe for tourists, good nightlife in P. Burgos.
Intramuros — Spanish colonial walled city, Fort Santiago, Rizal Museum, best for history, not for staying overnight.
Poblacion (Makati) — Hipster neighbourhood, craft beer bars, cheap street food alongside trendy restaurants.
areasToAvoid: Tondo and North Manila at night — highest crime areas in the city, no tourist reason to be there.
Quiapo market area after dark — pickpocket-heavy even during the day.
Accepting rides from unlicensed vehicles outside the airport or malls.
scamWarnings: Airport taxi scam: unlicensed drivers outside NAIA terminal claim all taxis are gone. Use the official metered taxi queue inside the terminal.
Short-changing: carefully count change at markets and smaller shops — tourist short-changing is common.
Overpriced jeepney tourist rides: locals pay ₱12–15 for a jeepney. If someone is charging you ₱200, that's a tourist rate.
Card skimming at ATMs: use ATMs inside banks or major malls only.

airportTransfer.cheapest: Airport Bus Express (P2P Bus) from NAIA Terminal 3 to Ayala Ave (Makati) — ₱60, takes 30–60 min. Air-conditioned, reliable.
airportTransfer.fastest: Grab car from NAIA — ₱300–500 to BGC or Makati, takes 20–40 min.
airportTransfer.recommended: Grab app is the most reliable option from any NAIA terminal. Book inside the building using the app.
airportTransfer.taxiEstimate: Official metered airport taxi: ₱350–600 to BGC or Makati depending on terminal and traffic. Use only the official taxi bay.

tippingEtiquette: 10% tip at restaurants is standard. Check if service charge is already included.
Filipino culture values warmth and respect above all — smile, say 'po' and 'opo' (respectful yes) to earn genuine hospitality.
Filipinos are extremely English-fluent — no need for translation apps in tourist areas.
Respect religious observances — the Philippines is strongly Catholic. Masses are frequent and observed seriously.

dailyCostBreakdown.accommodation: Budget guesthouse ₱600–1,200 | Mid-range hotel ₱2,000–4,500 | Boutique hotel ₱5,000–10,000
dailyCostBreakdown.meals: Jollibee meal ₱100–150 | Turo-turo (cafeteria) ₱60–100 | Restaurant dinner ₱350–700 | BGC restaurant ₱700–1,500
dailyCostBreakdown.activities: Intramuros tour ₱300–500 | National Museum free | Rizal Park free | BGC street art walking free

packingTips: Light, breathable clothing — Manila is hot and humid year-round (28–32°C).
Rain jacket for June–November — typhoon season can bring sudden heavy downpours.
Portable charger — traffic jams and malls mean long days away from outlets.
Modest clothing for churches — many Filipinos attend mass daily and dress appropriately.
premium.bookingStrategy: December–May is dry season — the best time to visit. Book accommodation in BGC or Makati for the safest and most convenient experience. Manila is often a transit city — allow 2–3 days before flying to El Nido or Palawan.
localTips: Grab is non-negotiable — Manila traffic is some of the worst in Asia, and the app shows you estimated time and price upfront.
Lechon (whole roasted pig) at any fiesta or market is the Filipino feast dish — Cebu-style is considered the gold standard.
The National Museum of Fine Arts and the National Museum of Natural History are both free and excellent.
Greenbelt mall in Makati has the best concentration of quality Filipino restaurants and brands.
Jeepneys are an iconic Filipino experience — colourfully decorated mini-buses, ₱12–15 for any journey within a route. Worth trying once.
premium.bestTimeToVisit: January–March: dry, relatively cool (26–28°C), low typhoon risk, Christmas festivities winding down.
dayTrips: Tagaytay — 2 hours south, Taal Volcano in a lake in a lake, cool temperatures (10°C lower than Manila), excellent bulalo beef stew.
Corregidor Island — 90 min by boat, WWII Pacific battlefield, dramatic history, panoramic Manila Bay views.
Las Piñas Bamboo Organ — 45 min south, unique 200-year-old church with an organ made of bamboo pipes, still played today.
visaInfo: Citizens of the US, UK, EU countries, Canada, and Australia: visa-free entry for 30 days on arrival — valid passport with at least 6 months validity and a return ticket required
The 30-day stay can be extended to a maximum of 36 months in 2-month increments at the Bureau of Immigration in Manila (Intramuros) — each extension costs PHP 3,030
Return or onward ticket is required at check-in by most airlines flying to Manila — book a flexible return to avoid issues at check-in
Manila is served by Ninoy Aquino International Airport (NAIA) — one of four terminals; confirm your terminal when booking
Immigration may ask for proof of sufficient funds ($50 USD per day is the guideline)
currencyTips: Cards accepted at major Manila malls (SM, Ayala), hotels, and restaurants; cash essential for jeepneys, tricycles, local eateries (turo-turo), and wet markets
Best ATMs: BDO, BPI, and Metrobank ATMs are widely available in Metro Manila and work reliably with foreign Visa/Mastercard; fees run PHP 200–250 per transaction
Metrobank and BPI tend to have the most reliable foreign card acceptance across the Philippines
Avoid exchanging money at NAIA airport (poor rates) — use the airport ATMs or exchange offices in Makati/BGC
premium.simCard: Globe Telecom and Smart/TNT are the two dominant carriers. DITO Telecommunity is a growing third option. Buy a SIM at NAIA airport arrivals or at any mall (Globe and Smart have kiosks in all major malls). A tourist SIM with 8–20 GB costs PHP 350–600. Globe has better 4G coverage in the Visayas and Boracay; Smart tends to be better in Mindanao and more remote areas. eSIM via Airalo is available for the Philippines.
